name: context-rotation
description: Automatic context rotation for Claude Code. At ~65% context usage a PreToolUse hook interrupts and has the agent write a detailed ROTATION-HANDOVER.md (decisions + working context, not just a task log); SessionStart re-injects it after /clear. /long-horizon (or CONTEXT_ROTATION_LONG_HORIZON=1 for one session) arms full auto-rotation (tmux-driven /clear) across a chain of sessions.

prerequisites.name: python3
prerequisites.install_hint: Install Python 3 (macOS: xcode-select --install or brew install python; Linux: apt-get install python3)

install._check_note: Intentionally 'false' so wire.sh ALWAYS runs on install — it is idempotent and re-syncs hook edits. A file-exists check would skip updates.

post_install_hints: Restart Claude Code so the PreToolUse / PostToolUse / SessionStart hooks load.
Default (always on): at ~65% context you'll be interrupted to write ROTATION-HANDOVER.md, then run /clear — the next session auto-loads it.
Run /long-horizon to arm hands-off auto-rotation (requires tmux for the automatic /clear); /long-horizon-off to disarm.

Shortcut: `lh` opens a new tmux window running `claude --dsp` with long-horizon armed for that launch only (no shell env leak). `lh 75` also sets the threshold. Restart your shell after install to pick it up.

Windows: core rotation (detect + handover + re-inject) works in Git Bash, but hands-off auto-/clear needs tmux, which Git Bash lacks — run unattended long-horizon sessions in WSL. See references/wsl-setup.md for the one-time WSL setup (native node+claude, auth, sessions/config sharing with the Windows store).
Tune ~/.claude/hooks/context-rotation/config (CR_WINDOW, CR_THRESHOLD) or override per-shell with CONTEXT_ROTATION_WINDOW / CONTEXT_ROTATION_THRESHOLD.
